author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
God Not Only Foreknows that Men Will Be Good, But Himself Makes Them So.
title: God Not Only Foreknows that Men Will Be Good, But Himself Makes Them So.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: It is He Himself, therefore, that makes those men good, to do good works.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: For He did not promise them to Abraham because He foreknew that of themselves they would be good.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: For if this were the case, what He promised was not His, but theirs.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: But it was not thus that Abraham believed, but “he was not weak in faith, giving glory to God;” and “most fully believing that what He has promised He is able also to perform.”

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: He does not say, “What He foreknew, He is able to promise;” nor “What He foretold, He is able to manifest;” nor “What He promised, He is able to foreknow:” but “What He promised, He is able also to do.”

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: It is He, therefore, who makes them to persevere in good, who makes them good.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: But they who fall and perish have never been in the number of the predestinated.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: Although, then, the apostle might be speaking of all persons regenerated and living piously when he said, “Who art thou that judgest another man’s servant?

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: To his own master he standeth or falleth;” yet he at once had regard to the predestinated, and said, “But he shall stand;” and that they might not arrogate this to themselves, he says, “For God is able to make him stand.”

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: It is He Himself, therefore, that gives perseverance, who is able to establish those who stand, so that they may stand fast with the greatest perseverance; or to restore those who have fallen, for “the Lord setteth up those who are broken down.”
